title: 6-wake up
html:
TL;DR

  • May 10 2020, 1:20pm Apple Note from 18-year-old Yahir mid-pandemic era in GP: a 45-min morning-routine block — "6,6:15 stretch / 6:15,7 read / 7,7:15 shower". Implicit 6 wake-up (note title). Classic manifesto style note form: bare timestamps, action verbs, no commentary.
  • This is a stretch-read-shower stack — body, mind, body — that prefigures Yahir's later supplement stack / non negotiables for traveling habit-architecture notes. Earliest dated micro-routine in the corpus.
  • COVID context: school remote, no commute, runway to redesign the morning. The note is Yahir at 18 reaching for no runway discipline and the max effort commit register the corpus later cements in 2021–2022.
  • Read (no specifics) sits between physical anchors — reading-as-routine-input rather than reading-as-project. Sibling intent to the later Ram Dass / Sisyphus reading list note 18 months on.
  • The 15-min granularity is the same template as later daily plans Yahir builds for peru prep 2021 travel windows.

Source quote

"6,6:15- stretch
6:15,7 - read
7,7:15 - shower"

Open questions

  • Did Yahir actually run this routine, or write it and bail? No corroborating dated evidence in May–June 2020 corpus.
  • 7:15-onward — the note ends mid-morning. Was the rest of the day un-planned, or planned in a sibling note that didn't survive export?
  • Is this the first dated wake-up-at-6 commit, or are there earlier ones in teen era notes?

Wikilinks

related.label: Distinguishing your self doesn't mean getting ahead, bringing something to the table of value is what gets you ahead
